steps.work: A degree (°) is a unit for measuring angles. A full turn around a point is 360°.
steps.explanation: Just like inches measure length, degrees measure how much an angle opens.

steps.instruction: What are the key angle benchmarks?
steps.work: Right angle = 90°. Straight angle = 180°. Full turn = 360°.
steps.explanation: These three benchmarks help you estimate and compare any angle.

steps.instruction: An angle measures 135°. Compare it to the benchmarks.
steps.work: 135° > 90° (greater than a right angle). 135° < 180° (less than a straight angle).
steps.explanation: This angle is between a right angle and a straight angle — it opens more than a square corner but less than a straight line.

title: Finding Angles as Fractions of a Full Turn

steps.instruction: A full turn is 360°. Find 1/4 of a full turn.
steps.explanation: A quarter turn is a right angle.
A half turn is a straight angle.
Three quarter turns equal 270°. You can also think of it as 360° − 90° = 270°.

steps.instruction: The minute hand moves from 12 to 9. What angle has it turned?
steps.work: 12 to 9 is 3/4 of the clock face = 3/4 × 360° = 270°.
steps.explanation: Clocks are a great way to visualize angle fractions. Each quarter of the clock is 90°.
